api/README.html.summary: API Documentation Directory This directory is auto-generated by DocFX during the build process. How It Works When you run docfx docfx_project/docfx.json from the repository root, DocFX will: Scan the C# projects specified in docfx.json (configured to look for src/**/*.csproj) Extract XML documentation comments from your code Generate API reference documentation in this directory Create a toc.yml file that organizes the API documentation Important Notes Do not manually edit generated DocFX output files in this folder (such as *.yml and toc.yml) -- they will be overwritten each time you run the DocFX build Hand-authored files like index.md and this README.md are intentionally maintained by hand and will be preserved across DocFX runs The actual API reference metadata files (*.yml files) will be generated automatically Namespace The generated API documentation covers the Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient namespace and its sub-namespaces.

api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbClientOption.title: Class DbClientOptions | Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbClientOption.summary: Class DbClientOptions Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Assembly Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.dll Process-wide knobs for Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient. Settings here are read from the global Dapper type-map delegate that ColumnAttributeTypeMapper installs, so they affect read-side column resolution only. public static class DbClientOptions Inheritance object DbClientOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Scope today: Applies to DbExtractor<TRecord> — its static constructor triggers the type-map registration that consults these settings. Only takes effect for TRecord types that have at least one ColumnAttribute; types without any [Column] use Dapper's built-in name matcher, which is not routed through this options class. Does not affect DbLoader<TRecord>: loaders emit parameters from a POCO, they don't read result-set columns. Set the flags once at process startup, before constructing the first extractor for a given TRecord, for the most predictable behavior. Properties StrictColumnMapping Controls how the library reacts when a SELECT result-set column does not map to any property on the destination TRecord type. public static bool StrictColumnMapping { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks false (default) — silently ignore the unmapped column, matching Dapper's out-of-the-box behavior. Common with SELECT * and joins that include columns the POCO doesn't need. true — throw an InvalidOperationException naming the offending column and the target type. Useful while developing to catch typos in [Column] attributes the moment they happen. The flag is read on every column lookup, so flipping it at runtime takes effect for the next query without re-registering type maps.

api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbColumnAttrib.summary: Class DbColumnAttribute Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Assembly Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.dll Optional per-property override for a DbTableAttribute-decorated record. Maps the annotated property to a column whose name differs from the property name, or marks it as Skipped from the generated SQL. api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbExtractor-1..title: Class DbExtractor<TRecord> | Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbExtractor-1..summary: Class DbExtractor<TRecord> Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Assembly Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.dll Extracts records from a database query as an asynchronous stream. Uses Dapper for column-to-property mapping, supporting [Column] attribute and convention-based name matching. public class DbExtractor<TRecord> : ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>, IExtractWithProgressAndCancellationAsync<TRecord, DbReport>, IExtractWithCancellationAsync<TRecord>, IExtractWithProgressAsync<TRecord, DbReport>, IExtractAsync<TRecord>, IAsyncDisposable, IDisposable where TRecord : notnull Type Parameters TRecord The POCO type representing a single row. Properties are mapped from result set columns by name or [Column("name")] attribute. Inheritance object ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport> DbExtractor<TRecord> Implements IExtractWithProgressAndCancellationAsync<TRecord, DbReport> IExtractWithCancellationAsync<TRecord> IExtractWithProgressAsync<TRecord, DbReport> IExtractAsync<TRecord> IAsyncDisposable IDisposable Inherited Members ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ExtractAsync() ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ExtractAsync(CancellationToken) ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ExtractAsync(IProgress<DbReport>) ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ExtractAsync(IProgress<DbReport>, CancellationToken) ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CreateProgressTimer(IProgress<DbReport>) ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ExtractWorkerAsync(CancellationToken) ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CreateProgressReport() ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.IncrementCurrentItemCount() ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.IncrementCurrentSkippedItemCount() ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.DisposeAsync() ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.Dispose() ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.Dispose(bool) ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.StartedAt ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.Elapsed ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ReportingInterval ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CurrentItemCount ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CurrentSkippedItemCount ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.MaximumItemCount ExtractorBase<TRecord, DbReport>.SkipItemCount object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The caller owns the DbConnection lifetime — the extractor does not open, close, or dispose it. The connection must be open before calling ExtractAsync. An optional DbTransaction can be provided for isolation level control. The extractor never commits or rolls back the transaction. Thread safety. A DbExtractor<TRecord> instance is not safe for concurrent ExtractAsync calls. Internal state (stopwatch, total-count snapshot, progress-counter increments) assumes a single extraction in flight. Build a separate instance per concurrent extraction. Command timeout uses the Dapper/ADO.NET default (typically 30 seconds). A dedicated CommandTimeout property is planned (see GitHub issue #25). Constructors DbExtractor(DbConnection, DbTransaction?, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbExtractor<TRecord> that auto-generates a SELECT statement from [Table] and [Column] attributes on TRecord. public DbExtractor(DbConnection connection, DbTransaction? transaction = null, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters connection DbConnection An open DbConnection. The caller owns its lifetime. transaction DbTransaction An optional DbTransaction for isolation control. logger ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException connection is null. InvalidOperationException TRecord does not have a [Table] attribute. DbExtractor(DbConnection, string, IDictionary<string, object>, DbTransaction?, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbExtractor<TRecord> with a parameterized SQL command. public DbExtractor(DbConnection connection, string commandText, IDictionary<string, object> parameters, DbTransaction? transaction = null, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters connection DbConnection An open DbConnection. The caller owns its lifetime. commandText string The SQL query to execute. parameters IDictionary<string, object> Named parameters for the query. A defensive copy is taken at construction time, so mutations to the supplied dictionary after construction do not affect the executed query or the values reported in debug logs. transaction DbTransaction An optional DbTransaction for isolation control. logger ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException connection, commandText, or parameters is null. DbExtractor(DbConnection, string, DbTransaction?, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbExtractor<TRecord> with a SQL command. public DbExtractor(DbConnection connection, string commandText, DbTransaction? transaction = null, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters connection DbConnection An open DbConnection. The caller owns its lifetime. commandText string The SQL query to execute. transaction DbTransaction An optional DbTransaction for isolation control. logger ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException connection or commandText is null. DbExtractor(DbProviderFactory, string, string, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbExtractor<TRecord> that owns the connection's lifetime. The connection is created from the supplied DbProviderFactory, opened lazily before extraction begins, and disposed when extraction completes (or throws). public DbExtractor(DbProviderFactory factory, string connectionString, string commandText, ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters factory DbProviderFactory The provider-specific factory (e.g. Microsoft.Data.SqlClient .SqlClientFactory.Instance, Npgsql.NpgsqlFactory.Instance). connectionString string The provider-specific connection string. commandText string The SQL query to execute. logger ILogger<DbExtractor<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException factory, connectionString, or commandText is null. InvalidOperationException factory returned a null connection from CreateConnection(). Properties CommandText The SQL command text being executed. public string CommandText { get; } Property Value string CommandTimeout How long each command (the extraction query and the TotalCountQuery) may execute before timing out. null (the default) means "use the ADO.NET provider's default", which is typically 30 seconds. public TimeSpan? CommandTimeout { get; set; } Property Value TimeSpan? Remarks Maps onto Dapper's commandTimeout parameter (an int? count of seconds). Fractional seconds in the supplied TimeSpan are truncated. A negative TimeSpan is rejected. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException The assigned value is negative. CommandType How CommandText is interpreted by the ADO.NET provider. Defaults to Text (a SQL statement). Set to StoredProcedure to invoke a stored procedure by name; CommandText then holds the procedure name. public CommandType CommandType { get; set; } Property Value CommandType Remarks TableDirect is supported by very few providers (notably OleDb). It's accepted on this property — Dapper passes it through — but most consumers should stick to Text or StoredProcedure. DefaultTotalCountQuery The default total count implementation. Wraps CommandText in SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (...) AS _count and executes it using the same connection, parameters, and transaction as the extraction query. Assign this to TotalCountQuery to enable the built-in behavior. public Func<CancellationToken, Task<int>> DefaultTotalCountQuery { get; } Property Value Func<CancellationToken, Task<int>> Remarks Trailing semicolons are stripped automatically. If the command text contains an ORDER BY clause, some database providers (e.g. SQL Server) may reject it inside a derived table. Use a custom TotalCountQuery in that case. ManageConnection When true, the extractor opens the connection before the first command runs and closes it after the enumeration ends. The connection is NOT disposed — it's returned to the pool for reuse, which plays better with connection-pool lifetime in web apps and hosted services. public bool ManageConnection { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks Default false preserves the v0.4.0 behavior: the caller is responsible for opening the connection before calling ExtractAsync. Ignored on the owned-connection ctor path (the (DbProviderFactory, connectionString, …) overload). That path always manages and disposes the connection because it created it. If the connection is already open when ExtractAsync starts, it's left open — the extractor only closes connections it itself opened. PagingClauseTemplate SQL fragment appended to the command text when both ServerOffset and ServerLimit are set. Bound as Dapper parameters @PageOffset and @PageLimit. public string PagingClauseTemplate { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks Defaults to LIMIT @PageLimit OFFSET @PageOffset — the SQLite / PostgreSQL / MySQL syntax. For SQL Server, set to OFFSET @PageOffset ROWS FETCH NEXT @PageLimit ROWS ONLY (and ensure the base SQL ends with an ORDER BY). Parameters Optional override for the parameter set passed to Dapper. Setting this property takes precedence over any IDictionary<string,object> supplied via the constructor — useful when the command is a stored procedure with OUT / INOUT parameters that need to be declared with ParameterDirection values Dapper can't infer from a plain dictionary. public DynamicParameters? Parameters { get; set; } Property Value DynamicParameters Remarks Caller-owned: the extractor never clones it. After ExtractAsync completes, read output values via Parameters.Get<T>("@name"). Example: var p = new DynamicParameters(); p.Add("@CustomerId", 42); p.Add("@TotalCount", dbType: DbType.Int32, direction: ParameterDirection.Output); var extractor = new DbExtractor<Order>(conn, "usp_GetOrdersForCustomer") { CommandType = CommandType.StoredProcedure, Parameters = p }; var orders = await extractor.ExtractAsync().ToListAsync(); var total = p.Get<int>("@TotalCount"); ServerLimit Page size in rows. See ServerOffset. public long? ServerLimit { get; set; } Property Value long? ServerOffset When both ServerOffset and ServerLimit are set, the extractor appends PagingClauseTemplate to the command text before sending it. Default null disables server-side paging (the v0.4.0 behavior — the full result set comes back and SkipItemCount/MaximumItemCount filter client-side). public long? ServerOffset { get; set; } Property Value long? Remarks Use server-side paging for very large tables where streaming everything to the client is wasteful. SQL Server requires an ORDER BY in the command text for paging to be deterministic; SQLite, PostgreSQL, and MySQL don't require it but you should still include one — without a stable order, page contents drift. TotalCountQuery When non-null, this function is invoked before extraction begins to determine the total record count, which is then reported via Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.Report.TotalItemCount. Assign DefaultTotalCountQuery to use the library's built-in SELECT COUNT(*) subquery, or supply a custom function for a more efficient query. Defaults to null (total count is not fetched). public Func<CancellationToken, Task<int>>? TotalCountQuery { get; set; } Property Value Func<CancellationToken, Task<int>> Methods CountAsync(CancellationToken) Runs the configured TotalCountQuery (or the built-in DefaultTotalCountQuery if none is assigned) and returns the result. Useful when the caller wants the total count without actually streaming the rows — for example, sizing a progress bar before kicking off the extract. public Task<int> CountAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken Token to cancel the count query. Returns Task<int> The row count reported by the underlying query. Remarks Doesn't mutate any state on the extractor — does not touch DbReport.TotalItemCount, the stopwatch, or any of the progress counters. Safe to call any number of times before, during (different cancellation token), or after an ExtractAsync. Opens the connection on the owned-connection ctor path before running the query and disposes it after — same lifecycle as a full extraction. CreateProgressReport() Creates a progress report of type TProgress. This gives the derived class the opportunity to implement a custom progress report that is specific to the extraction process. protected override DbReport CreateProgressReport() Returns DbReport Progress of type TProgress CreateProgressTimer(IProgress<DbReport>) Creates the Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.IProgressTimer used to drive progress callbacks. Override this method in a derived class to inject a custom timer (for example, a custom implementation that allows manual control in unit tests). protected override IProgressTimer CreateProgressTimer(IProgress<DbReport> progress) Parameters progress IProgress<DbReport> The progress sink that will receive callbacks. Returns IProgressTimer A started Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.IProgressTimer instance. ExtractWorkerAsync(CancellationToken) This method is the core implementation of the extraction logic and should be overridden by derived classes. protected override IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord> ExtractWorkerAsync(CancellationToken token) Parameters token CancellationToken A CancellationToken to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord> IAsyncEnumerable<TSource> The result may be an empty sequence if no data is available or if the extraction fails.

api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbLoader-1.htm.title: Class DbLoader<TRecord> | Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.DbLoader-1.htm.summary: Class DbLoader<TRecord> Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Assembly Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.dll Loads records into a database via INSERT or UPDATE commands. Uses Dapper for parameter mapping from POCO properties. public class DbLoader<TRecord> : LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>, ILoadWithProgressAndCancellationAsync<TRecord, DbReport>, ILoadWithProgressAsync<TRecord, DbReport>, ILoadWithCancellationAsync<TRecord>, ILoadAsync<TRecord>, IAsyncDisposable, IDisposable, ISupportDryRun where TRecord : notnull Type Parameters TRecord The POCO type representing a single row. Properties are mapped to command parameters by name or [Column("name")] attribute. Inheritance object LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport> DbLoader<TRecord> Implements ILoadWithProgressAndCancellationAsync<TRecord, DbReport> ILoadWithProgressAsync<TRecord, DbReport> ILoadWithCancellationAsync<TRecord> ILoadAsync<TRecord> IAsyncDisposable IDisposable ISupportDryRun Inherited Members LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.LoadAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord>) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.LoadAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord>, CancellationToken) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.LoadAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord>, IProgress<DbReport>) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.LoadAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord>, IProgress<DbReport>, CancellationToken) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CreateProgressTimer(IProgress<DbReport>) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.LoadWorkerAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord>, CancellationToken) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CreateProgressReport() LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.IncrementCurrentItemCount() LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.IncrementCurrentSkippedItemCount() LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.DisposeAsync() LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.Dispose() LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.Dispose(bool) LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.StartedAt LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.Elapsed LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.ReportingInterval LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CurrentItemCount LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.CurrentSkippedItemCount LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.MaximumItemCount LoaderBase<TRecord, DbReport>.SkipItemCount object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Two transaction modes are supported: Caller-managed — pass a DbTransaction to the constructor. The loader uses it but never commits or rolls back. The caller is responsible for transaction lifetime. Auto-managed — pass null for the transaction parameter. The loader creates its own transaction, commits on success, and rolls back on exception. The caller owns the DbConnection lifetime — the loader does not open, close, or dispose it. The connection must be open before calling LoadAsync. Thread safety. A DbLoader<TRecord> instance is not safe for concurrent LoadAsync calls. Internal state (stopwatch, progress counters) assumes a single load in flight. Build a separate instance per concurrent load. Command timeout uses the Dapper/ADO.NET default (typically 30 seconds). A dedicated CommandTimeout property is planned (see GitHub issue #25). Constructors DbLoader(DbConnection, string, DbTransaction?, ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbLoader<TRecord> with a custom SQL command. public DbLoader(DbConnection connection, string commandText, DbTransaction? transaction = null, ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters connection DbConnection An open DbConnection. The caller owns its lifetime. commandText string The SQL INSERT or UPDATE command to execute per record. transaction DbTransaction An optional DbTransaction. If null, the loader creates and manages its own transaction (commit on success, rollback on failure). logger ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException connection or commandText is null. DbLoader(DbConnection, WriteMode, DbTransaction?, ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbLoader<TRecord> that auto-generates an INSERT statement from [Table] and [Column] attributes on TRecord. public DbLoader(DbConnection connection, WriteMode writeMode, DbTransaction? transaction = null, ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters connection DbConnection An open DbConnection. The caller owns its lifetime. writeMode WriteMode Determines whether to generate INSERT or UPDATE commands. transaction DbTransaction An optional DbTransaction. If null, the loader creates and manages its own transaction. logger ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException connection is null. InvalidOperationException TRecord does not have a [Table] attribute, or writeMode is Update and no [Key] properties exist. DbLoader(DbProviderFactory, string, string, ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>>?) Initializes a new DbLoader<TRecord> that owns the connection's lifetime. The connection is created from the supplied DbProviderFactory, opened lazily before loading begins, and disposed when loading completes (or throws). public DbLoader(DbProviderFactory factory, string connectionString, string commandText, ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>>? logger = null) Parameters factory DbProviderFactory The provider-specific factory (e.g. Microsoft.Data.SqlClient .SqlClientFactory.Instance, Npgsql.NpgsqlFactory.Instance). connectionString string The provider-specific connection string. commandText string The SQL INSERT or UPDATE command to execute per record. logger ILogger<DbLoader<TRecord>> An optional logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException factory, connectionString, or commandText is null. InvalidOperationException factory returned a null connection from CreateConnection(). Properties BatchCommitSize Commit the auto-managed transaction every N successfully-loaded rows. Defaults to 0, meaning "commit only once at the end of the load" (the v0.4.0 behavior). Larger values let very long loads survive a mid-flight failure with most of the work persisted. public int BatchCommitSize { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks Only takes effect when the loader manages the transaction (i.e. the caller did NOT pass a DbTransaction to the constructor) AND BatchSize is 1. With a caller-supplied transaction the loader never commits, so this knob has no effect; with BatchSize > 1 the per-batch commit boundary is the BatchSize's own batch, which we currently don't subdivide. Trade-off: a partial-progress failure with BatchCommitSize > 0 leaves earlier committed batches in the destination. You lose all-or- nothing semantics in exchange for resumability and lower undo-log pressure on the database. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException The assigned value is negative. BatchSize Number of records sent per ExecuteAsync call. Defaults to 1 (one round-trip per record). Larger values pass an IEnumerable<TRecord> to Dapper, which amortizes per-call overhead (parameter parsing, command setup) and lets the ADO.NET provider reuse the prepared command across rows. public int BatchSize { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks On networked databases (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MySQL) this is typically the largest single performance lever in the loader. Memory cost is one buffered List<T> of up to BatchSize records at a time. SkipItemCount and MaximumItemCount are still honored at the per-record granularity — skipped records never enter the batch buffer, and buffering stops accepting new items once the cumulative-plus-buffered count would exceed MaximumItemCount. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when the assigned value is less than 1. CommandText The SQL command text being executed per record. public string CommandText { get; } Property Value string CommandTimeout How long each ExecuteAsync call may run before timing out. null (the default) means "use the ADO.NET provider's default", typically 30 seconds. public TimeSpan? CommandTimeout { get; set; } Property Value TimeSpan? Remarks Maps onto Dapper's commandTimeout parameter (an int? count of seconds). Fractional seconds are truncated. Applies to every batch, not the whole load — so a batched insert of 10 batches each gets the full timeout independently. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException The assigned value is negative. CommandType How CommandText is interpreted by the ADO.NET provider. Defaults to Text (a SQL INSERT / UPDATE). Set to StoredProcedure to invoke a stored procedure by name per record (or per batch when BatchSize is > 1); CommandText then holds the procedure name. public CommandType CommandType { get; set; } Property Value CommandType CurrentErrorCount Number of rows that have failed to load so far (incremented for every Skip-handled exception). Resets to 0 at the start of each LoadAsync call. public int CurrentErrorCount { get; } Property Value int ErrorHandling How the loader reacts when a single row's ExecuteAsync throws. Defaults to Abort, which preserves the v0.4.0 behavior (first failure rolls back the transaction and propagates). public RowErrorHandling ErrorHandling { get; set; } Property Value RowErrorHandling Remarks Skip only takes effect on the per-record path (BatchSize == 1, the default). A failed batch cannot be safely attributed to a single row without per-item retry, so the load still aborts even in Skip mode when BatchSize > 1. See Skip. InsertBatchSize When greater than 1, the loader replaces N per-row INSERTs with a single multi-row INSERT … VALUES (…), (…), … statement. The single biggest single-line perf win available without provider-specific bulk APIs. public int InsertBatchSize { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks Contract on CommandText: it MUST end with the literal token VALUES followed by a parenthesized parameter template such as (@FirstName, @LastName, @Age). The loader extracts that template, replicates it once per row in the batch with suffixed parameter names (@FirstName_0, @FirstName_1, …), and rebinds each row's properties to its slot via reflection. Database parameter limits apply — SQL Server caps at 2,100 params per command, SQLite at 999, MySQL much higher. Choose InsertBatchSize so that InsertBatchSize × columns stays under your provider's limit. Default 1 preserves the v0.4.0 behavior (one statement per row, or N statements bundled via BatchSize). Mutually exclusive with BatchSize > 1 — when both are set, InsertBatchSize wins because it generates a single statement per chunk instead of N. Mutually exclusive with IsDryRun = true (IsDryRun short-circuits before SQL generation) and with stored-procedure CommandType. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException The assigned value is less than 1. IsDryRun When true, the loader runs the full pipeline — enumerates the source, evaluates SkipItemCount / MaximumItemCount, increments progress counters, fires the progress-timer callback, and emits all the usual log messages — but skips the actual ExecuteAsync call. The database is not modified. public bool IsDryRun { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks Useful for validating an ETL pipeline (source feed, mapping, batching, throttling) against production without writing anything, or for estimating how long a write would take. The auto-managed-transaction path still BeginTransaction / Commits — those are connection-level operations and are needed for the open/dispose lifecycle. They have no effect on the database because no writes happen inside the transaction. Default false preserves the prior behavior. This is the implementation of Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.ISupportDryRun.IsDryRun from Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions 0.15.0+. ManageConnection When true, the loader opens the connection before the first command runs and closes it after the load ends. The connection is NOT disposed — it's returned to the pool for reuse, which plays better with connection-pool lifetime in web apps and hosted services. public bool ManageConnection { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks Default false preserves the v0.4.0 behavior: the caller is responsible for opening the connection before calling LoadAsync. Ignored on the owned-connection ctor path (the (DbProviderFactory, connectionString, …) overload). That path always manages and disposes the connection because it created it. If the connection is already open when LoadAsync starts, it's left open — the loader only closes connections it itself opened. MaxErrorCount Maximum number of row-level failures the loader will tolerate before aborting the load with an InvalidOperationException whose InnerException is the last underlying row failure. Defaults to 0, meaning "unlimited" (every failure is reported via RowFailed and processing continues). public int MaxErrorCount { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks Only consulted when ErrorHandling is Skip. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException The assigned value is negative. Methods CreateProgressReport() Creates a progress report of type TProgress. This gives the derived class the opportunity to implement a custom progress report that is specific to the loading process. protected override DbReport CreateProgressReport() Returns DbReport Progress of type TProgress CreateProgressTimer(IProgress<DbReport>) Creates the Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.IProgressTimer used to drive progress callbacks. Override this method in a derived class to inject a custom timer (for example, a custom implementation that allows manual control in unit tests). protected override IProgressTimer CreateProgressTimer(IProgress<DbReport> progress) Parameters progress IProgress<DbReport> The progress sink that will receive callbacks. Returns IProgressTimer A started Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.IProgressTimer instance. LoadWorkerAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord>, CancellationToken) This method is the core implementation of the loading logic and should be overridden by derived classes. protected override Task LoadWorkerAsync(IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord> items, CancellationToken token) Parameters items IAsyncEnumerable<TRecord> The items to be loaded to the destination. token CancellationToken A CancellationToken to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks Items may be an empty sequence if no data is available or if the loading fails. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Argument items is null Events RowFailed Raised once per row that fails when ErrorHandling is Skip. The handler receives the failing record, the underlying exception, and the row's 1-based item index. public event EventHandler<RowFailedEventArgs<TRecord>>? RowFailed Event Type EventHandler<RowFailedEventArgs<TRecord>> Remarks Typical use: capture the failing record into a dead-letter store. The handler is invoked synchronously inside the load loop, so a slow handler will slow the entire load.

Triggers the Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient source generator to emit a partial class with reflection-free Insert SQL + parameter-binding helpers. Attribute.IsDefined(ParameterInfo, Type, bool) Attribute.Match(object) Attribute.TypeId object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The target type MUST be declared partial so the generator can add members to it. Every public property maps to a column of the same name unless overridden with DbColumnAttribute. First v0.5.0 cut: Type-level analysis only — the generator emits a public const string Insert and a static void Bind(Dapper.DynamicParameters parameters, T record) helper. Future versions will expand to Update, Delete, and Select with proper integration into DbExtractor / DbLoader. Constructors DbTableAttribute(string) Initializes a new DbTableAttribute. public DbTableAttribute(string name) Parameters name string Table name as it appears in the database. Used as-is, no quoting — the caller is responsible for any provider-specific identifier escaping (square brackets, double quotes, backticks). Properties Name The table name supplied to the constructor. public string Name { get; } Property Value string

api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.RowErrorHandli.title: Enum RowErrorHandling | Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.RowErrorHandli.summary: Enum RowErrorHandling Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Assembly Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.dll Controls how DbLoader<TRecord> reacts when a single row's ExecuteAsync call throws. public enum RowErrorHandling Fields Abort = 0 First failure rolls back the auto-managed transaction (if any) and propagates the exception out of the load. This is the default and matches the v0.4.0 behavior. Skip = 1 Log/event the failure via DbLoader.RowFailed, advance CurrentErrorCount, and continue processing the next row. If DbLoader.MaxErrorCount is non-zero and the error count reaches it, the load aborts with an InvalidOperationException referencing the threshold and the last underlying exception is included as the inner exception. Skip mode only applies on the per-record path (BatchSize == 1, the default). When BatchSize > 1, a failed batch can't be reliably attributed to a single row without per-item retry, so the load aborts even in Skip mode. Set BatchSize = 1 if row-level error handling is required.

api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.WriteMode.html.title: Enum WriteMode | Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.WriteMode.html.summary: Enum WriteMode Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Assembly Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.dll Specifies the type of SQL command the DbLoader<TRecord> auto-generates from attribute metadata. public enum WriteMode Fields Insert = 0 Generate an INSERT statement. All non-identity-key columns are included. Update = 1 Generate an UPDATE statement. Requires at least one property decorated with [Key] for the WHERE clause.

api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.html.title: Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient | Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient.html.summary: Namespace Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient Classes DbClientOptions Process-wide knobs for Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient. Settings here are read from the global Dapper type-map delegate that ColumnAttributeTypeMapper installs, so they affect read-side column resolution only. DbColumnAttribute Optional per-property override for a DbTableAttribute-decorated record. Maps the annotated property to a column whose name differs from the property name, or marks it as Skipped from the generated SQL. DbExtractor<TRecord> Extracts records from a database query as an asynchronous stream. Uses Dapper for column-to-property mapping, supporting [Column] attribute and convention-based name matching. DbLoader<TRecord> Loads records into a database via INSERT or UPDATE commands. Uses Dapper for parameter mapping from POCO properties. DbReport Progress report for ADO.NET extraction and loading operations. Extends the base Wolfgang.Etl.Abstractions.Report with database-specific context. DbTableAttribute Marks a record / class as mapping to a database table. Triggers the Wolfgang.Etl.DbClient source generator to emit a partial class with reflection-free Insert SQL + parameter-binding helpers. RowFailedEventArgs<TRecord> Payload for DbLoader{TRecord}.RowFailed. Fires once per row that failed to load when ErrorHandling is Skip. Enums RowErrorHandling Controls how DbLoader<TRecord> reacts when a single row's ExecuteAsync call throws. WriteMode Specifies the type of SQL command the DbLoader<TRecord> auto-generates from attribute metadata.
